title: hypochaeris glabra subsp. arachnoides
intro: Hypochaeris glabra subsp. arachnoides, also known as lesser hawk's beard or sticky mouse ear, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which can help to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. The medicinal properties of Hypochaeris glabra subsp. arachnoides are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which can provide several health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal medicine, Hypochaeris glabra subsp. arachnoides is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion to promote healing and overall well-being.
